sentences.text: Task 1.
You are going to listen to five texts.
For each of them, answer the two questions given.
Mark the correct answer A, B, or C.
You have 20 seconds to look through the task.
You will hear the recording twice.
Text 1.
I want to tell you about my neighbor Jane.
Jane is a student, but she also works in a shop.
She doesn't like her job,
because she doesn't earn enough money.
Jane needs a better job, because she has to pay for her studies herself.
Unfortunately, neither her mother nor her uncle can help her pay for her studies,
as neither of them work.
The other day, she had job interviews at a supermarket, and a hotel,
but she wasn't able to get a job.
But luckily, the job interview Jane had yesterday in a bank, went well.
She is looking forward to their decision at the moment.
She hopes, she'll get the job.
If Jane starts working at the bank, she won't have to worry about her university fee.

The first private art gallery in post-Soviet Tbilisi, the Beja Gallery, was established in
1993. The gallery exhibits works by young artists, as well as previously unknown pictures by well-known
painters. On the birthday of the outstanding Georgian painter Elena Akhvlediani, the gallery
displayed a selection of previously unseen paintings, collected from the individual
collectors. Elena Akhvlediani is best known, for her paintings of Georgian towns, for her
illustrations in books by Georgian writers, and for designing sets for the Marjani Shwili
theater plays. The visitors had a chance not only to view Elena Akhvlediani's masterpieces,
but also to buy some of the displayed paintings. The exhibition held at the Beja Gallery,
presented Elena Akhvlediani's paintings created from 1920 to the 1970s.

Text 3. Elias, the new language teacher at a Finnish primary school, is a very patient teacher.
Elias can repeat questions many times, and never gets out of the way.
Don't be surprised, Elias is not a human, it is a robot teacher.
Unlike usual experienced teachers, Elias can even dance at the lessons.
It has special equipment, that allows it to encourage schoolchildren, to learn new things.
It gives feedback to teachers about a schoolchild's possible problems.
The robot also recognizes the child's skill levels,
and changes its questions.
Accordingly, the robot is able to understand, and speak 23 languages.
Some of the human teachers see it, as a new way to engage children in learning.
Despite its skills in languages and mathematics, however, the robot is not able to keep discipline
among primary school children. It means that, for the time being at least, the human teachers
are not at risk, to lose their jobs.

Text 4
Samuel Pepys was one of the world's greatest writers of diaries.
For almost 10 years, Pepys recorded his daily life.
He frankly described all his weaknesses,
that many men wouldn't admit even to themselves.
Apart from recording his own life, Pepys wrote about the world around him,
giving a detailed description of life in London in the 1660s,
including the Great Fire of London, and the pandemics of plague, a very serious disease.
Publishing a diary can help you make money if you're a celebrity or a politician.
However, the best reason for keeping a diary is that, it can be great to remember an event in the future.
While your memory can keep only a limited amount of information, a diary can record all the details,
and remind you of people and events that you'd forgotten about.
After the Bible and James Boswell's life of Samuel Johnson,
Pepys' Diary is the best bedside book in the English language.

For almost 10 years, Pepys recorded his daily life.
He frankly described all his weaknesses,
that many men wouldn't admit even to themselves.

Text 5. There are many funny facts connected with Harvard University.
It refers to Johnson's
gate 2, which is considered the main gate of the university. Despite being the main gate,
it is closed for most of the year. Harvard University has many other gates, which are
open 24 hours every day. So, anyone could visit the campus anytime. But Harvard students should
pass through Johnston Gate only twice. Once, when they first arrive at the university campus during
their freshman year.
And a second time after they graduate. It's considered bad luck if they go through it more
than those two times. Another fact is connected with the campus. During their first year at the
university, students are required to live on campus. However, they can move out when they
become second or third year students. So naturally, all famous people who attended Harvard University
live in the dormitory, which is a building where students live. Mark Zuckerberg, the founder of
Facebook, was not an exception either. Text 6. There are many funny facts connected with Harvard

Task 2. You are going to listen to one text, with eight questions. Mark the correct answer,
A, B, or C. You now have 30 seconds to look through the task. You will then hear the recording twice.
Hi, I'm David, your tour guide in London. I've lived and worked in this amazing city,
all my life. Yes, I'm a proud Londoner, born and bred.
I really enjoy helping visitors get the best out of their trip to London,
which I believe is one of the best cities in the world.
Samuel Johnson, the author of the first English dictionary in the 1750s, said,
If a man is tired of London, he is tired of life, and I can honestly say I am not yet tired.
There are so many things to do and see in London.
The short story of London's name goes like this. When the Romans invaded Britain in 43 AD,
they founded a city on the banks of the River Thames and called it Londinium.
Soon Londinium became the largest city in Roman Britain, which remained under Rome's authority
until 410 AD. We are now in the area which was established by the Romans 2,000 years ago.
It's now called the City of London, but it is also referred to simply as the City.
The City is the most ancient historic part of London from which the rest of London
developed. It is the most ancient part of London from which the rest of London developed.
There are still traces of Roman London all over this area.
Did you know that Londinium had a Roman amphitheater where gladiators and wild animals fought?
Hard to believe, right? Well, follow me to discover some more surprising things about London.
Now, let's take a walk around this ancient part of London called the City.
It's very small, only 1.1 square miles, but it's one of the richest financial
districts in the world. There are more Japanese banks in this 1.1 square mile than there are in
Tokyo, and there are more American banks here than there are in Manhattan. Have a look at the
buildings. This particular part of London is famous for its perfect mix of old and new
architecture. Now we're approaching Sir Christopher Wren's Monument to the Great Fire of London of 1666.
Sir Christopher Wren was Britain's most famous architect. He was 33 years old,
and was just starting out on his career as an architect when the Great Fire of London destroyed
many of the city's public buildings. Sir Christopher Wren built 51 churches after the
Great Fire of London. 21 of those churches still exist today, including his incredible masterpiece,
St. Paul's Cathedral. Now here's the Monument to the Great Fire of London,
which is more commonly known as the Monument. Take a closer look at the Monument while I tell
you more about it.
On the 2nd of September 1666, in a small bakery in Pudding Lane, a baker named Thomas Farinor
forgot to put out the fire in his oven before going to bed. As a result, the Great Fire of
London began. A strong wind caused the fire to spread fast. Within five days, 80% of the old
city of London was burnt down. The gold tip of the monument is at a height of 62 meters,
which is the exact distance from the
monument to the baker's shop in Pudding Lane where the fire started. If you're feeling
energetic, you can take 311 steps to the top of the monument to enjoy one of the best views
over the city and take amazing photos. They'll even give you a certificate for reaching the top
of the monument. Lastly, we're headed for London Bridge. The Romans built a bridge over the River
Thames on this site 2,000 years ago, and there's been a London Bridge in the same area for the
longest time. It's a bit windy around here on the bridge, but get your cameras ready for the best
sights of London. Looking into the distance on your left, over the River Thames, you can see the
most magnificent view of the incredible Tower Bridge of London. And with this, we finish our
